Title: Engineering hormonal crosstalk to enhance 5-HT/MT accumulation in rice without compromising yield

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts in ABA–5-HT feedback signaling by dephosphorylating SAPK2, thereby reducing ABI5 phosphorylation and attenuating ABA-induced 5-HT biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive nutrients; MT is a derivative of 5-HT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split luciferase complementation; BiFC; Co-IP; in vitro pull-down; in vitro dephosphorylation assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPP2C30 physically interacts with SAPK2. 2. OsPP2C30 dephosphorylates SAPK2 and suppresses SAPK2-mediated ABI5 phosphorylation. 3. It is part of the ABA-signaling node through which 5-HT feedback attenuates ABA-induced 5-HT biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct interaction with ABI5 was supported for PP2C30; effect on ABI5 is indirect via SAPK2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to weaken negative feedback and elevate 5-HT/MT, but direct engineering evidence here focused mainly on OsPP2C51 knockout.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts in ABA–5-HT feedback signaling by dephosphorylating SAPK2, thereby reducing ABI5 phosphorylation and attenuating ABA-induced 5-HT biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive nutrients; MT is a derivative of 5-HT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split luciferase complementation; BiFC; Co-IP; in vitro pull-down; in vitro dephosphorylation assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPP2C49 physically interacts with SAPK2. 2. OsPP2C49 dephosphorylates SAPK2 and suppresses SAPK2-mediated ABI5 phosphorylation. 3. It contributes to the feedback module by which 5-HT attenuates ABA signaling.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct interaction with ABI5 was supported for PP2C49; effect on ABI5 is indirect via SAPK2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to tune ABA feedback and increase 5-HT/MT, though not directly engineered in this study.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator in ABA–5-HT feedback; dephosphorylates SAPK2 and thereby lowers ABI5 phosphorylation, attenuating ABA-induced 5-HT biosynthesis; knockout was used to enhance grain 5-HT/MT
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive nutrients; MT is a derivative of 5-HT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split luciferase complementation; BiFC; Co-IP; in vitro pull-down; in vitro dephosphorylation assay; CRISPR knockout; grain metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPP2C51 interacts with SAPK2 and dephosphorylates it. 2. OsPP2C51 inhibits SAPK2-mediated ABI5 phosphorylation. 3. CR-OsPP2C51 was part of the engineering strategy to elevate 5-HT/MT in rice grains.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prior work cited direct ABI5 interaction for PP2C51; this study used CR-OsPP2C51 and a combined pUbi::OsABI5/CR-OsPP2C51 line in engineering.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced ABA signaling/background may suppress seed germination
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to relieve feedback repression and increase 5-HT/MT, especially in combination with ABI5 overexpression.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Central ABA-signaling kinase that phosphorylates ABI5 to promote 5-HT biosynthetic gene activation; overexpression was used to engineer higher 5-HT/MT, though with slight yield decrease
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive nutrients; MT is a derivative of 5-HT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; Co-IP; split luciferase complementation; BiFC; bacterial kinase assay; in vitro kinase assay; LC-MS/MS phosphosite mapping; overexpression; grain metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSAPK2 interacts with ABI5 and phosphorylates ABI5. 2. T162 is essential for SAPK2 autophosphorylation and ABI5 phosphorylation, while T283 supports ABI5 interaction and phosphorylation. 3. 5-HT likely attenuates ABI5 phosphorylation via the PP2Cs–SAPK2 node. 4. OsSAPK2 overexpression was included as an engineering strategy for elevating 5-HT/MT.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although mechanistically central, OsSAPK2 is a signaling kinase rather than a transcription factor or metabolic enzyme; classified here as Common Gene.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NGDC BioProject PRJCA046995; phosphoproteomic site identification by LC-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression resulted in a slight decrease in yield; enhanced ABA signaling also reduced germination
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to raise 5-HT/MT through stronger ABA signaling, but may require balancing to avoid yield/germination penalties.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA receptor component whose ABA-induced interaction with PP2C30/49/51 is antagonized by 5-HT, thereby influencing downstream SAPK2–ABI5 signaling and 5-HT biosynthesis feedback
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive nutrients; MT is a derivative of 5-HT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-IP; tobacco transient expression interaction assay under ABA and 5-HT treatments
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA promotes PYL5 interaction with PP2C30/49/51. 2. 5-HT attenuates the ABA-induced PYL5–PP2C interaction. 3. This provides a mechanistic entry point for feedback inhibition of ABA-induced 5-HT biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mechanistically but not directly engineered for metabolic enhancement in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling target for future tuning of ABA–5-HT crosstalk.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increasing ABA biosynthesis through overexpression elevated endogenous ABA and supported engineering of higher grain 5-HT/MT
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive nutrients; MT is a derivative of 5-HT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ubi-driven overexpression; ABA quantification in grains; cooked grain metabolite profiling; agronomic trait analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNCED1 overexpression increased ABA in grains. 2. ABA elevation was used as an engineering route to enhance 5-HT/MT accumulation. 3. Most ABA-enhanced lines maintained major yield traits but had reduced germination.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown mature plants; cooked grain analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced seed germination associated with elevated ABA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering ABA biosynthesis genes is a practical route to elevate 5-HT/MT while largely preserving yield.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increasing ABA biosynthesis through overexpression elevated endogenous ABA and supported engineering of higher grain 5-HT/MT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ubi-driven overexpression; ABA quantification in grains; cooked grain metabolite profiling; agronomic trait analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNCED2 overexpression increased endogenous ABA. 2. Enhanced ABA was used to promote 5-HT/MT accumulation in grains.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown mature plants; cooked grain analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced seed germination associated with elevated ABA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate ABA-biosynthesis engineering target for nutritional enhancement.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increasing ABA biosynthesis through overexpression elevated endogenous ABA and supported engineering of higher grain 5-HT/MT; this line also showed increased yield
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ubi-driven overexpression; ABA quantification in grains; cooked grain metabolite profiling; agronomic trait analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNCED3 overexpression increased ABA in grains. 2. It was part of the successful engineering strategy to enhance 5-HT/MT. 3. Unlike most lines, the OsNCED3 overexpression line showed increased yield.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown mature plants; cooked grain analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced germination due to elevated ABA, despite favorable yield outcome
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target because it improved target metabolites and reportedly increased yield.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increasing ABA biosynthesis through overexpression elevated endogenous ABA and supported engineering of higher grain 5-HT/MT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ubi-driven overexpression; ABA quantification in grains; cooked grain metabolite profiling; agronomic trait analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNCED4 overexpression increased ABA. 2. It was used as an ABA-metabolism engineering entry point to improve 5-HT/MT.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown mature plants; cooked grain analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced seed germination associated with elevated ABA

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNCED5 overexpression increased ABA. 2. It was included among ABA-metabolism engineering targets to elevate 5-HT/MT.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown mature plants; cooked grain analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced seed germination associated with elevated ABA

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout of ABA catabolism gene increased endogenous ABA and supported engineering of higher grain 5-HT/MT
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; ABA quantification in grains; cooked grain metabolite profiling; agronomic trait analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Disrupting OsABA8ox1 elevated grain ABA. 2. Reduced ABA catabolism was used to increase 5-HT/MT accumulation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown mature plants; cooked grain analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced seed germination associated with elevated ABA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing ABA catabolism is a useful route for 5-HT/MT biofortification.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes 5-HT and downstream MT biosynthesis; transcription is induced by ABA through ABI5
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream precursor pool for 5-HT and MT
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme for 5-HT synthesis (as stated for TDC/T5H)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR after ABA treatment; ABI5 promoter binding by CUT&Tag and EMSA; dual-luciferase transactivation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pyridoxal phosphate-dependent aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase family
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsTDC1 is transcriptionally induced by ABA. 2. ABI5 directly binds its promoter and activates transcription. 3. Upregulation of OsTDC1 contributes to enhanced 5-HT/MT synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway gene for increasing 5-HT/MT, but the paper emphasizes regulatory engineering to avoid growth penalties from direct TDC manipulation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes 5-HT and downstream MT biosynthesis; transcription is induced by ABA through ABI5
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream precursor pool for 5-HT and MT
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme for 5-HT synthesis (as stated for TDC/T5H)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR after ABA treatment; ABI5 promoter binding by CUT&Tag and EMSA; dual-luciferase transactivation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pyridoxal phosphate-dependent aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase family
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsTDC3 is transcriptionally induced by ABA. 2. ABI5 directly binds its promoter and activates transcription. 3. Upregulation of OsTDC3 contributes to enhanced 5-HT/MT synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway gene for increasing 5-HT/MT, but the paper emphasizes regulatory engineering to avoid growth penalties from direct TDC manipulation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes 5-HT biosynthesis from tryptamine and thereby elevates downstream MT; overexpression increased endogenous 5-HT and reduced ABA sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream to direct product-forming step for 5-HT
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product 5-HT and supplies MT biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR after ABA treatment; ABI5 promoter binding by CUT&Tag and EMSA; dual-luciferase transactivation; overexpression; metabolite profiling; ABA-response phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsT5H is strongly induced by ABA. 2. ABI5 directly binds the OsT5H promoter and activates transcription. 3. Overexpression of OsT5H increases endogenous 5-HT and confers reduced ABA sensitivity. 4. OsT5H is a central rate-limiting step for 5-HT/MT enrichment.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for elevating 5-HT/MT; also useful as a tool to test feedback crosstalk with ABA.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP family; ABI5/ABF-type ABA-responsive transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly activates OsTDC1, OsTDC3, and OsT5H to promote ABA-induced 5-HT biosynthesis and increase downstream MT accumulation; overexpression elevated 5-HT/MT
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive nutrients that support sleep, anxiety reduction, and immune modulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; phosphorylation-dependent activation and stabilization
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates transcription of OsTDC1, OsTDC3, and OsT5H
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; SAPK2-mediated phosphorylation; antagonistic feedback from 5-HT via PP2Cs–SAPK2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in negative feedback loop where 5-HT attenuates ABA signaling, reduces SAPK2-mediated ABI5 phosphorylation, lowers ABI5 activity/stability, and suppresses further 5-HT biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening; CUT&Tag; EMSA; dual-luciferase transactivation; CRISPR knockout; overexpression; qPCR; metabolite profiling; in vivo phospho-assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts within ABA signaling module with SAPK2 and PP2Cs; direct DNA binding to target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced ABA signaling associated with reduced germination; overexpression used for nutritional engineering may require balancing with feedback regulators
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsABI5 was identified as the strongest promoter-binding TF for OsTDC1/OsTDC3/OsT5H. 2. It directly binds and activates these 5-HT biosynthetic genes. 3. Overexpression increases 5-HT/MT, while CRISPR knockout weakens ABA induction of pathway genes. 4. SAPK2-dependent phosphorylation enhances ABI5 activity, whereas 5-HT feedback suppresses ABI5 phosphorylation and stability.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phosphomimic S44D enhanced transcriptional activity; 5-HT reduced ABI5 protein accumulation without significantly changing ABI5 transcript under ABA.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive ABA-responsive transcriptional activator linking hormone signaling to 5-HT pathway gene induction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major engineering node for raising 5-HT/MT; especially effective when combined with weakening of feedback repression (e.g., CR-OsPP2C51).
